receiver_rule: Trial-wave outcomes are bounded self-improvement results. They may inform future patches or parking decisions, but they do not become governance truth, identity truth, or hot-memory authority.

candidate_record.baseline_story: Consumer shells historically risked drifting on short-board meaning, closeout reading, surface-versioning summaries, and fallback order.
candidate_record.candidate_story: The bounded consumer contract, surface versioning, and drift-validation wave now keep the same first-hop story across Codex-style, Claude-style, dashboard, and observer consumers.
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: shared interpretation fracture while one shell still looks cleaner locally
candidate_record.rollback_path: revert packaging/readout changes and fall back to Tier-1 session-start ordering
candidate_record.overclaim_to_avoid: aligned packaging is not improved reasoning or governance quality
candidate_record.scope_limit: packaging and shared interpretation only; no reasoning-runtime or identity change

analyzer_closeout.result_story: Consumer-parity packaging now reads as aligned across the bounded shells that matter most.
unresolved_items: alignment is packaging-level only, not proof of better reasoning
future consumer changes must keep running the same drift wave
analyzer_closeout.promotion_limit: does not authorize council, identity, or transport-semantics changes
analyzer_closeout.overclaim_warning: cross-consumer alignment is not outcome calibration or self-improvement of cognition
analyzer_closeout.next_action: reuse this drift-validation wave whenever shared consumer packaging changes

result_surface.carry_forward_rule: may inform future consumer-packaging trials, but does not authorize runtime, governance, or identity promotion

candidate_record.baseline_story: Operator retrieval had boundaries and landing-zone ideas, but no final query contract and no safe carry-forward posture for later operator use.
candidate_record.candidate_story: The operator-retrieval contract now defines query classes, provenance-first return shape, and non-promotion rules so retrieval can stay auxiliary.
candidate_record.success_metric: operator_retrieval_contract_present and compiled_landing_zone_spec_present and live runner maturity
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: retrieval reading as runtime truth before a real compiled corpus and safe runner exist
candidate_record.rollback_path: revert retrieval-facing packaging and keep retrieval in planning-only posture
candidate_record.overclaim_to_avoid: a query contract is not a live retrieval system or solved memory layer
candidate_record.scope_limit: operator-facing retrieval packaging only; no runtime truth or identity promotion

analyzer_closeout.result_story: Operator retrieval packaging is now bounded and safer to reason about, but it is not live enough to promote as an active capability.
analyzer_closeout.evidence_bundle_summary: operator retrieval contract present; compiled landing-zone spec present; no live retrieval runner
unresolved_items: no live operator-retrieval runner is in the mainline yet
no compiled corpus or collection health lane exists yet
no operator validation wave has tested retrieval against real use
analyzer_closeout.promotion_limit: does not authorize retrieval answers to outrank session-start, observer-window, packet, or identity surfaces
analyzer_closeout.overclaim_warning: operator-retrieval packaging is not solved knowledge memory and not runtime authority
analyzer_closeout.next_action: revisit only after a live bounded retrieval runner and compiled collections exist

result_surface.carry_forward_rule: may guide future retrieval packaging, but does not authorize retrieval answers to outrank runtime truth
